channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 220 replies — 880.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 220 • Updated: 2026-07-10 23:27 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62455990
>>62455475
Look into VPMAX

62456048
>>62455990
Neat

62459187
>>62403053
The median 64 yo is planning to work until they're dead.
Boomers are never going to retire because they can't afford to. They are going to keep working like the good little goyim they are and fall down dead on the job because that's the only way they can fund the monthly payments on all the toys they went into debt to purchase.
I spit on their graves. Fuck boomer scum.

62459238
>>62459187
Lmao, another white incel who knows everything,,,,,what are the odds? 100% at least,,,,,do they use it to get rich? Lmao no

62460695
Yawwwwwn another week another VOO purchase

62460733
>>62460695
i made a whole $5 buying a share of VOO during that dip :) too bad I lost more from all my previous VOO shares

62460835
>>62460733
Sorry to hear that champ maybe DRAM is more your speed

62460844
>>62400995
Lmfao. You do realize that the highest weighted stocks that lead the spy are mostly in QQQ right? Lmfao

62460852
>>62459187
They can afford it

62461238
>>62401051
>I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?
It changes significantly depending on when you enter the market. Since 2000 there's only a 0.6% difference in favor of QQQ over SPY, the latter outperformed the former for most of that timeframe. If you bought sometime post-2009, however, you would have been better off holding QQQ by far. The moral of the story is keep an eye on things and only buy more volatile assets if it's a growth period. QQQ will continue to be great if you bought a decade ago until the music stops.

62461412
>>62400515
Fpbp. Bogleheads are the biggest retards on the planet.

62461672
>>62400620
>What makes one rich
Health, family, friends.

62462476
>>62461238
For now I’m still just doing VOO cause it just feels better even if I’m missing out on qqq gains

62464487
It’s payday tomorrow, VOO brahs. There’s nothing risky that looks too appealing so I go all voo maybe some palantir as well. My only regret is taking the work health benefits, my check will be smaller. Two weeks is an eternity to wait for your money.

62464830
Is there any point of voo and chill if I already have a 401k and pension. Know can compound it more but can be more risky right

62466388
>>62400493
buy lottery tickets

62467200
>>62466388
But VOO, QQQM if you want more return and volatility

62467206
>>62460835
no no i don't buy dogshit. thanks

62467215
>>62464830
retire earlier
imagine if you have enough VOO in your brokerage that you can afford to live off its dividends only. you'll pay virtually no tax and enjoy 10% growth per year indefinitely.

62467367
>>62467206
I was being sarcastic I’m a Boglehead

62468767
>>62461672
Am i rich if i have health, family, and friends but i'm stuck wageslaving 50 hours a week and perpetually stressed and taking orders like a bitch and doing endless donkey work just to watch my bosses and clients get more money and freedom than me?

62468775
>>62400493
Buying voo only makes sense if you have size.

62468793
>>62468775
Is 7”x5” enough?

62468838
Reckless strategy. Practically gambling. Do you even ACWI IMI?

62468882
>>62468767
The great majority of people need to work to live. And there are many miserable "rich" people and many happy "poor" people. You will come to learn.

62468885
How much are you guys DCA'ing into the shit and piss 500? I'm doing about $2,500 every monthy and I'm at a little over $780k.

62468889
>>62468885
Idk 12% of my paycheck for however long it's been I don't look at it

62468891
>>62468885
Also, I have noticed that the people complaining about the returns from the S&P500 are people who don't have a job and only have a lump sum to invest.

62468920
i got 100k saved up from wageslaving, how do invest it to chill and earn with etf's?

also should i buy these etf directly from vanguard?

62468957
>>62400636
This was me at 35 just doing the bare minimum to match at my company. It wasn’t 150 but 280 but you get the idea.

I’m now 39 and maxing out my HSA/Roth IRA/Roth 403b/bonds. It’s close to 60k a year I’m putting way and I still have 20 years to catch up.

It can be done but so many of my peers are barely saving and living above there means it’s crazy.

item.title: Why aren’t more anons cops? — 45 replies — 180.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren’t more anons cops?

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-07-10 23:22 UTC

OP

You can abuse and arrest non whites, and if they shoot you and kill you then you successfully KYS’d without needed to pull the trigger yourself. It’s literally killing two birds with one stone potentially.

Early replies (first 3)

62465528
>>62465494
I was thinking about it right around the end of high school and I came to the conclusion that I'd rather join the military if I had to. For reference, I'm a Euro (non-western). In the end I just finished a tech uni and got an office job.

62465552
>>62465528
I didn’t even know there were euro police officers. I thought the entire point of Europe was to get raped in the ass by Arabs and Africans with HIV. Pepe’s are obviously American cops in the meme. So fuck off.

62465568
I did it. Quit after 5 years. It’s full of NPC’s, politics, lesbians, backstabbers, militaryfags, and buying into the “thin blue line” propaganda. You will only work with cops, date other cops, fuck other cops, get divorced and become and alcoholic. Life expectancy is 3-5 years after retirement. I was a good officer. I saved someone’s life and didn’t get any recognition because I was fucking the prized female senior officer.

I hate cops more than ever now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62466175
>>62465494

62466186
>>62465494

62466189
>>62465899
There's no way you can get away with this. Maybe 1. Possibly 2 but after that a supervisor will be on your ass. The supervisors hate having to tell customers your package was stolen because it looks bad on them to their boss.

62466250
>>62465494
Cops are forced to deal with the most low IQ retarded scum of the earth on a daily basis and if they dare harm a hair on any one of these token niggers, the media, ngos, and faggot politicians will throw them under the bus in an instant.
No thanks.

62467124
>>62466117

All Cops are horny. If you spend enough time with the right person, you will eventually fuck them. This is true in any job with a female and male dynamic. I think it happens more in LE because it’s a very closed off work group. Over 5 years I “dated” 2 other female officers and had the option to fuck the department bicycle. Both were solid 7’s with a 8.5 almost a 9 if she had an ass. 2 were Latina and 1 white. White female was about 5’10 and top tier lover. She knew how to be sexy, flirty, slutty, submissive, and gorgeous when dressed up. We went to Vegas a few times together. Every time she would get dressed to gamble down in the casino, security would constantly ask for her I.D., assume she was a working girl, or accuse me of being a sex trafficker. You don’t see many blonde women that are 6 foot+ in high heels wearing the tightest black dress known to man. I miss her so much, it hurts sometimes.

62467136
>>62465494
Literally dealing with the lowest of the low for sub 100k take home pay. You’d have to be a retard to be cop which most are

62467147
>>62465494
>risking being killed by crazed groids for $100k/year
>make "the wrong call" and your name will be in the news, receive death threats, maybe even lose your job or end up in prison

62467149
>>62467124
>I miss her so much, it hurts sometimes.
did you guys end it on okay terms? ambiguous terms?

62467214
>>62467149

I got another woman pregnant. I without a doubt made the wrong choice. This was a hard truth to swallow at first. I can only openly admit to this to myself when I’m alone with my thoughts.

I was full of myself for a while because you wear a badge and have “power”. I was also fucking random thots off social media as well. Being an officer is a pussy cheat code as long as you aren’t a fucken retard manlet. I would without a doubt trade all the other women for her. But I have a son now and I can’t take that back.

62467226
>>62465568
Maybe don’t fuck your colleagues. Retard

62467250
>>62467226
Nobody wants to date cops, so they have to get with each other

62467266
>>62467250
>>62467226

Nobody wants to date them, but they do want to fuck them.

62467354
>>62467214

62467387
>>62467354

Imagine being in the grocery store and just being hit with an avalanche of pain and regret because you grabbed an item off the shelf and it reminded you of her. Probably the gayest and saddest feelings ever conceived.

62467411
>>62465494
yeah, it's called extrajudicial killing/suicide by cop. There's a reason why usa (and texas in particular) have so many. and surprise, surprise retard smooth brain trailer trash, it affects whites the most lmao. you're retarded and your mom sucked a million dicks

62467435
>>62467387
U should play Disco Elysium , ur Harry irl

62467448
>>62465552
We need cops because otherwise who is going to enforce the humiliation ritual.

62468118
>>62465568
>I was fucking the prized female senior officer.
Fucking colleagues is based beyond belief

62468155
>>62467136
This

I make $150k to click some spreadsheets and shitpost in my underwear at home. I make a ton of mistakes too and no one cares lol.

62468378
>>62465494
I'm not stupid enough, and I'm not smart enough that I can pretend to be stupid

62468422
>>62468118

They would purposely separate us on shift. She sucked my dick on shift twice in the locker room on night shift. I worked for a small department so there was only 5-8 officers on shift including supervisors.

62468482
I almost fell for it, glad I didn't.
The people who don't start shit and are normal humans are not the people you interact with.

62468510
>>62465901
>ak47 (not akm)
>clip
>and the rest
Confirmed noguns

62468513
>>62467147
>the "wrong" call
I've never ever seen any of the sensationalized "wrong" calls actually turn out to be wrong when all the evidence actually comes out

62468555
>>62468513
when i lived in an apartment i had a neighbor who was a city cop in a major US city. his name was in the media for killing two niggers who ran from him while committing a crime. they were actually separate incidents over a period of a few years. his name was publicly dragged through the mud, he was in the headlines of the local news, he probably received death threats, etc., and in addition to this he lost his job for a period of a few years, until the union helped him fight the case and get him re-instated.

62468591
>>62468555
Checked
Its shit like this that makes me certain the outcome will eventually be vigilantism after we're done defunding the police and people have had enough of the revolving door of a completely broken and ineffective prison system
Whilst I'm not really looking forward to that moment, it can't come soon enough. My fatigue is sky high

62468598
>>62465494
Bootlicker thread

62468859
>>62468155
Doing what? As a cop? How are you at home in your undies making $150k?

62468896
>>62468859
NTA but that's about what I make doing the same thing working in IT for a corpo (full wfh)

62468948
army and police are for losers who cant compete in the real world

item.title: Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production — 83 replies — 88.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-07-10 22:36 UTC

OP

DTCC is going live on-chain later this year.
Yet crypto is in the shitter, why?

Early replies (first 3)

62463146
>>62463144
Explain how it's bullish for crypto?

62463270
>>62463146
Both Swift and DTCC are going to be using a variety of public chains.

62463273
So SWIFT has their own singular chain. I thought it'd be that each bank has their own chain, connected to each other by CCIP. If it's all done on a single chain then it obviates the need for a lot of CCIP transactions, right?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62465020
>>62464853
how's this adem?
https://x.com/The_DTCC/status/2075280707203592232

be honest now, how much did you lose in Wormhole, LayerZero and LinkSol?
imagine sinking your money in literal SCAMS just to stick it to le EBIL linkies and walking away even poorer LMAO

62465065
>>62465020
I hold 1,500 since im a poorfag. Been on this board since 2022 and have seen so many posts about dates and how chainlink will finally rise in price like when they claimed CCIP would make it rise, november of last year because that guy from swift says that they are done piloting, sergey going to the fucking white house and now the clarity act. I dont believe anything until it actually happens, until then ill just keep buying when i can.

62465075
>>62463686
/Biz/ would be fun for a few days

62465077
>>62463552
Now that's what I'm talking about

62465091
>>62463144
>Agentic commerce

Either these buzzwords die or I become very wealthy, winwin

62465124
>>62463144
TOKEN NOT NEEDED

62465212
>>62463146
It’s bullish for chainlink, which is all that matters

62465220
>>62465212
nothing is bullish for chainlink sweetie
it is an eternal dump coin of dumpage

62465269
tick tock fuddies

62465459
>>62463144
>>62463348
everything to do with chainlink

62465696
>>62463428
It isn't though.

62465711
>>62463144
>kek, thanks for the free case study and stress-testing on blockchain networks, suckers!

62465827
Testimg

62465841
>>62463144
>Swift is rolling out its own blockchain
it isn't
a private chain is worthless/ meaningless

62465848
>>62463146
mood! It was supposed to be XRP but they just cucked ceypto and made their own token.

62465917
>>62463144

ICP and DOT

https://youtu.be/W2A53FoHnjs

62466023
>>62465065
I held 2500+ at one point and also went through a belief phase. I remember religiously playing the link rap song, genuinely catchy. Eric Schmidt and sergey fireside was the peak of my hopium and I remember saying to myself this was it, this had to have been it, I mean the CEO of Google in its heyday giving props to the dev of my bags, it felt better than 2016 Trump winning. Every link marine on /biz/ was hyping each other up. Holy fuck Eric Schmidt just told sergey he was a genius and this was the next industrial revolution. Smart contract assassins holy FUCK!

Then the dopamine faded and the price chugged on as usual and more and more breadcrumbs started spawning with more and more dates. Ended up selling all at around $18 thinking I'd slurp at single digits and finally make it but have yet to buy back, I don't think I have it in me to baghold for more dates desu

62466028
>>62465841
What part of
>not needed
is not getting through to you?

62466034
>>62463670
>No, but 4=4

62466154
Pornlayer peelers shedding at an extreme rate

62466163
i need more linkies

62466164
>>62465917
>ICP
I bet you do, buddy.

62466376
>>62465841
>>62465696
>>62465917
The Swift ledger is built specifically to be connected to many chains, private and public.
The future is multichain, haven't you heard?

You L1 maxis really need to wipe the shit from your eyeballs already.

62466383
>>62466028
what the fuck are you talking about
>>62466376
>built specifically to be connected to many chains, private and public
you only need 1 public chain for ordering, that's it
and since ordering is done there, the liquidity is also there
a private chain is an oxymoron because you don't need to order anything,you're not going to sandwich attack/double spend your own fucking transaction
this is apparently what xrpiglets didn't understand yet concerning this "tech" and are hopelessly illiterate on

62466401
>>62466383
>you only need 1 public chain

L1 maxi detected.
The future is multichain, how are you not wise to this yet holy shit.

62466434
it's all so tiresome
https://x.com/ChrisBarrett/status/1972765646976123278

62467985
>>62463144
Bearish for LINK unironically
>pilot

62467991
>>62466383
>you only need 1 public chain for ordering, that's it
This is impossible.

62467996
>>62467985
>live

cope with it

62468876
>>62463144
Link and swift are both trash and deserve each other.

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 12 replies — 24.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-07-10 23:02 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463754
It’s still on coingecko so you need to learn how to use google search correctly my friend

62464735
>>62461988
nobody is buying your bags bro and this MICA shit will not pump your very heavy bags lmaoooo

62465284
>>62461988
The flaw tih Everest is that you're trying to pump a project that supports regulations preventing P&D, with a "utility" token that has zero use outside being used as gas in beta test by one or two b2b customer, no fabled VNO until now as well, so where's the utility?

It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory, but it is also a last ditch attempt to make themselves more attractive to raise external funds, because there's no way they could obtain meaningful capital by selling more token at this price, so they get MICA to pitch it to institutional while it still warm; before inevitably many other similar project also obtain MICA and overtake Everest. Reminder that Everest has lost grounds on KYC, fiat on ramp, RWA products even to companies that don't use proprietary token.

62465296
>got it before Binance

oof

62465554
>>62465284
>It is clear that the MICA application was not because it is mandatory
Yeah anything you say can’t be taken seriously if you typed that shit

62465690
Everest > Binance

ID is priced into identity verification, eKYC/AML checks and payments, with a portion of each fee burned. Licensed exchanges lack identity stacks; identity protocols lack MiCA authorisation. Everest combines both: a demand structure most tokens lack.

62466022
How many tokens to make it?

62466677
>>62466022
0

62468922
0 as in G0ysl0p maxxer

item.title: FUCK JANNYS /CHEESE/ IS FOREVER SIEG HEIL — 5 replies — 7.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
FUCK JANNYS /CHEESE/ IS FOREVER SIEG HEIL

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-10 22:52 UTC

OP

FUCK JANNYS
/CHEESE/ IS FOREVER
SIEG HEIL

Early replies (first 3)

62468517
>>62468444
CHECKED and based

62468522
>>62468444
unbrielievably based

62468751
>>62468444
spammers like you should be hanged in the streets, kill yourself

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62468899
>>62468751
I’m not spamming you nigger FAGGOT

62468907
>>62468444
Fuck off curry faggot.

item.title: Pandemic meta — 3 replies — 7.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Pandemic meta

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-10 23:08 UTC

OP

Seeing as how the alt season failed, and chuds got left holding alt bags, it's not strange a man would adapt to the circumstances and find a new niche.. "news meta" . Basically what might occur in the news, whether hantavirus, a diarrhea outbreak or a parasite infestation in the US like Screwworm, solana and memecoins are still useful for quick gains that depend on narrative traction. Thoughts?

Early replies (first 3)

62468890
>>62468835
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YD3QKLOjKGM

62468908
>>62468835
Ok so what is your strategy?

62468927
>>62468835
memecoins are like 99.99% rugs and scams, so basically you have a 0.01% chance to make it with meme coins, nigga your better off just taking your money to a casino and bettering it all on a roulette number. trust me i found this out the hard way lmao

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 141 replies — 408.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 141 • Updated: 2026-07-10 23:11 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62458735
just end it bro

62458848
>>62449961
kind of fucked
your best bet if you have a car is to get a job working overnight at McDonalds or another 24 hour fast food place
if you aren't obviously a druggy or look like a psycho they will hire you on the spot, seriously
or just lie on your resume and get any menial wageslave job
just research some businesses that used to exist in your area, for example find restaurants that went out of business and say you worked as a dishwasher
if they ask about gaps in your resume you can say you were looking after a sick family member
make everything before the last 5 years or so a small business that you owned and operated yourself, for example lawnmowing

so your resume should look like this
graduated high school
worked for yourself as a believable small business like a lawnmower
"my business was destroyed by covid"
began looking after a sick relative for 2-3 years
worked as a dishwasher, or cleaning toilets, or some other thing for a business like a hotel that went out of business recently in your town or a nearby town. for 2 or so years until 6-12 months ago when that business closed
been searching for a job since then

problems with this method:
- if they check references you're fucked but no one does for these dead end jobs like McDonalds maintainence or dishwasher. there are services you can hire which pretend to be references for you but it shouldn't be necessary
- if you don't have a car you're just fucked, you need it to have a leg up on other applicants for these jobs. I'm pretty sure I was hired by McDonalds the moment I said I had my own vehicle and could be available to start at 2am
- if you get the job you're trapped in a hell dead end wage slave job

source: same situation as you OP but I was 29 rather than 39

62459478
>>62458735
Why, my life is better than yours.
>>62458848
I live miles from any McDonald's. I've got a convenience store, a place that used to be a bowling alley but turned into some other business I've never been in, OR a several mile trip along a freeway.

62460298
>>62449961
>>62449979
Some sort of low entry Zogbot job;
Coast Guard is easy
National Guard idk
Navy
Airforce

Avoid anything army related and you'll be alright.

t. Same boat but turned it around

62460390
op unironically save up enough money for a pressure washer and sign up for one of those indepdent contractor apps and start pressure washing boomers drive ways. save up enough to buy or lease a car then pivot into driving uber.


this is your only realistic path to making any kind of money beyond minimum wage

62460633
>>62460390
By driving for Uber? You okay man? You wanna talk about it?

62460654
>>62449961
Most strip malls have a staffing agency that would jump if anyone without a felony came in. It'll suck at the beginning but that's what you get for wasting 23 years of your life.

62460716
>>62460633
?

uber drivers crack $40/hour where I am

62460737
>>62460716
Well, that's unusual. They're getting very lucky.

62460759
>>62460737
I’d be surprised if they don’t make at least $25 an hour in most metro areas and the peripheries

where do you live if you don’t mind my asking that they are legitimately only making minimum wage?

62460789
>>62460759
>make $25 an hour
>spend double that on gas and vehicle maintenance.
How exactly is that supposed to pan out?

62460802
>>62460789
maintenance isn’t that much if you have a newer car, and I’m pretty sure driving some shit box is against uber rules anyway you need to register with a relatively new car


gas eh maybe you have a point but they’re still out so it’s obviously profitable

I highly doubt uber drivers are making minimum wage except in some super rural area where everyone had a car. And I know for a fact they make decent money where I live

62461444
>>62460802
So I need a high paying job to get a nice new car so I can ruin it working for a lower paying job?
I live in California, gas alone would fuck me sideways. If I had a nice car I'd leave. Go to Oregon or Washington.

62461781
I just realized I'm going to need to learn how to pay taxes.

62462300
>>62461781
https://www.irs.gov/individuals/free-tax-return-preparation-for-qualifying-taxpayers if you make less than 69k a year you probably qualify

62462365
trucking. self-driving trucks are a meme and still decades away, if you're a company driver you can pull down 80k clean with little overhead.

most companies will hire you on the spot if you have a CDL and a clean record

62462409
>>62459478
yeah you arre going to have to drive miles to your job
you DO have a car right op?

62462413
>>62462365
>if you have a CDL
they have pivoted to only hiring jeets as of a year or two ago

62462416
>>62460802
it's all jeets and other browns that cheat the system by having 3 drivers share the same car and uber account
you're lucky to make minimum wage after gas, tax and wear and tear

62462448
>>62462416
Actually, sharing a car makes sense. You at least split the cost of buying a car between you. And the insurance.

62463196
>>62462448
Triple the maintenance, but you have support from your other drivers

62464516
Is playing the lottery viable?

62465774
Sorry, I need the thread to stay up a little longer.

62465843
>>62449961
Similar to you OP, 40 years old, only 1 year office job experience.
Although I did learn programming in my free time. I am also taking care of me and my mother's estate which includes 3 rental properties and a sizable stock portfolio. I also taught myself ancient Greek, some Japanese, and some Russian. I read about business and finance daily.
I'm pretty sure I could be useful in an office setting, especially one finance or real estate related. Not necessarily because of my real estate or finance knowledge, but by being very comfortable using unix utilities, setting up servers, writing programs and scripts, and setting up web services. That, in addition to understanding, tax, finance, and renting.

I just don't know how to frame all these for an employer. I'm thinking it'd be easier to start an online agency.

62466261
>>62465843
You sound like you've got it made compared to me. Good luck on your agency.

62466338
>>62449961
Close your eyes.
Imagine a career.
Now make it into a resume.
There is no employment gap.

62468866
And, bump

62468886
Work should be vocational. Just do voluntary stuff that you feel is important or do your own thing. Anything else makes one miserable.

62468898
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2f4qnU1oOoc

62468936
>>62449961
I'm in a similarish position to this guy. I'm 38 and I'm thinking of becoming an actuary. I'm a math PhD dropout (have a master's I guess but I never bothered to apply for graduation) and I've basically been NEETing since. Work part time in a warehouse but that's not relevant to anything. Now someone my age switching to actuary isn't unheard of but usually they have a continuous record of experience in something that can give them an advantage, like being a teacher or w/e. I haven't taught since grad school which was a decade ago. I have nothing to show for my gap. Unironically how fucked am I? I have no chance of being hired recent grads because of my gap that only possibly be interpreted as laziness and apathy right? Passing the first round of exams would be easy but it costs money and it's likely for nothing if I don't get hired.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 248 replies — 191.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 248 • Updated: 2026-07-10 22:14 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464891
>>62464772
The haters are already watching Kek. They can’t stop talking about his wife in both threads and they’re literally reading court filings for his divorce.

62464895
>>62464891
They are convinced that Sonichu is both PP and ABC in disguise.

62464897
>>62464895
PP HAS to be every towel knower.

62464909
>>62464895
>everyone is PP
I am not PP, debunked. Shills can not dispute the holiday inn dilemma

62464919
>>62464772
$GABBBYQ has already won, this is why she hasn’t been since the 18th

62464943
I’m starting to think this is a larp

62464998
>>62464943
>The CUSIP Conundrum, the Director Dilemma, and the Affable Affiliates are all an elaborate ruse.

62465011
>>62464998
>The Gabbbyq Gape at the Holiday Inn
Interesting

62465133
>>62464943
What does live action role playing have to do with the bankruptcy of bed bath and beyond?

62465151
>>62464047
that's the thing about starting a religion. You have to figure out how to monetize it

so he creates a proxy for BBBY and then pretends it's being crucified by the romans just like your messiah was. Then he can trick some of you fools into buying it before rugging you like the dumbass sheep you are.

>>62464998
those things are obvious schizophrenic apophenia. What's not clear is if your schizophrenia is real or affected.

62465330
The shills were instructed to spam shit about the personal life of theppshow host because pulte(director of national intelligence) frequenting it and Larry cheng going on after shares were cancelled, were huge signals that told retail bbbyq is coming back.

Even more important is that the cusip-6 of bbbyq and the bbby warrants match. Shillies stopped having their mods frantically delete posts last fall/winter and now they just spam and flood to try and avoid the curiosity altogether.

62465397
>>62465330
Ok debunk the divorce filings in Utah and why Mrs PP has not been seen since the 18th and Ryan Cohen tweeted a T Rex image in the Bahamas

You can’t shilly willy

62465412
all they have left is forced satire and inauthentic contrived meme energy that never sticks which is why the AMC spam (attempt to discredit the CUSIP-6) stopped because this stuff never works and people see right through it.

62465418
>>62465412
silo the topic as much as you want, you can’t refute the holiday inn

62465422
>>62465418
I guess I'll take the bait.

You think focusing on the shows host is more important that the content discussed on the show? Or prominent figures who went onto the show who are very close to RC? Of course you don't because you're paid to be here.

62465431
>>62465422
I fully agree with you, Ryan has been inside the PP suite at the holiday inn as well, we all have. We are all waiting 9 months now for the divorce to conclude and we can all celebrate, at the holiday inn.

62465456
You'd think the shill sissies would be uploading these divorce documents or whatever the fuck they're talking about since they already uploaded PP's resume and everything else. Makes you wonder if they're just...making things up with zero basis whatsoever. Hard to blame them cause these boomers and jeets really can't meme. Between dougshit, CUSIP Conundrum, etc. they really have no way of controlling sentiment so they just make the lamest copies trying to capture any bit of meme energy.

62465498
>>62465456
Do your own research, the cuck chair rarely comes pre heated, but when it does, PP is there.

Funny how you haven’t disproven the (1) thing yet you refuse discuss, interesting

62465544
>>62465456

62465594
>>62465544
>no mention of his devoted wife
Divorce is ongoing, nothing happens until it concludes.

GME bulls have won

62465636
>>62465151
>your messiah
Kek

62466545
CUSIP-6

62466579
>>62466545
>ignores the real conundrum
GME Bulls are in control

62466875
>>62466579

62466937
>>62466875
>everything depends on GME bulls
>the GABBBYQ KEKS need fantasty RP for their theory

62467452
Hello friends. Are we tuning in to Jake's space call today? Should be lots of fun!

62467493
>>62467452
Thank you for the reminder, King! I almost forgot!

62467912
Jake2SNEED is the goat.

62468829
>>62467912
He is very based. No other stock community has anyone close to Jake. He is the bbbyq champion.

62468834
Nothing happens until the divorce is finalised and GABBBYQ has controlling interests on the board

GME bulls are in charge now

item.title: Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late. — 96 replies — 99.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Open thread • Replies: 96 • Updated: 2026-07-10 22:34 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62467805
>>62467798
Yes, yet people think Musk can do (or make his employees do) anything and do it better than the competition

62467819
>>62467796
At the end of the day this is well and good for China, but does nothing for the domestic US market and the tech companies within that will drive most of the demand for space hardware because these things are national security concerns. Basically America and China will each have their own domestic space industry and people will choose a provider based on who will not steal their tech. Take a guess.

62467824
Oh nice, took them long enough to steal the right data.

62467835
>>62467366
Shut the fuck up you chink worshipping faggot

62467844
>>62467824
ai is an arms race to steal as much data as possible from people the world over
chinese ai specifically is being catered to stealing all THAT data to parse it for advancement of the state apparatus (because commies). Its getting better at finding the better data now, but soon enough they'll just exhaust all the shit they can steal and nobody will want to innovate anymore and we'll have an age of ai hallucinated 'discovery' with predictably underwhelming (and often highly disastrous) results
Welcome to the chinese century

62467881
>>62466527
esl thread alert
>until it's too late
before it's too late

62468089
>>62467819
They are absolutely in direct competition. The main end product is connectivity, and that product is sold on the global market. Government contracts are only a fraction of SpaceX's revenue.

62468142
>>62468089
I didn't say government contracts. I'm talking about the deep link between government policy and corporate rollout. AI leadership is a national security concern and so companies like Nvidia and anthropic are deep in bed with the US government on what they can and can't do. See chip export restrictions and mythos lockdowns. So when the question becomes "which company do we trust to launch our highly proprietary hardware and models into space?" The answer is never ever going to be a Chinese company when forced IP disclosure is baked into the deal of working with Chinese state industries.
Same goes for China. They're making these rockets because they'll never in a million years leave their access to space gatekept by American foreign policy. The competition is on a government level and on foreign countries that want access to space. But that market is laughable. Only America and China have any real business in space and neither will buy from the other.

62468165
>>62466535
Thank you Elon very cool

62468190
>>62468142
Of course US companies will be banned from launching on Chinese rockets; this has already been the case since the 1990s.

The point is that Starlink will be in competition with China's Qianfan and Guowang constellations in third countries, which is the bulk of the connectivity market by value. China's reusable rockets are an input good into Qianfan and Guowang, since they will be used to launch en masse.

>forced IP disclosure is baked into the deal of working with Chinese state industries
No it isn't. It depends on the context and on the negotiating power of the Chinese company.

62468230
>>62468190
I disagree but time will tell.

62468297
>>62466527
>>62467881
Was going to point this out.

>Sell until it's too late
>"until it's too late"

Such a pathetically lazy ESL shill/FUD thread, but at least there's a learning opportunity here. Some things to note:

1) It's suspicious (and perhaps pathetic) how many people read the OP and responded in earnest without calling out the obvious ESL-slop OP.

2) It took what, like ~70 replies before someone called out the OP and pointed out the blatant ESL syntax?

3) There are several possibilities why this has happened, but three of the most probable are A) this board has been overrun with ESL retards who opine on American companies and American economics as if they know what they're talking about; B) blatant shill/FUD threads are started by foreigners and responded to by bots or engagement farms in order to boost signal in attempts to affect American investments/companies/assets/etc.; or C) a combination of A) and B).

4) This is why flags are so important. This board is gimped due to a lack of knowledge. We should be able to know whether the person giving us advice on American stocks is posting from Pakistan or Bulgaria or Ukraine. It's actually negligent to have a "business/finance" board without flags.

62468335
>>62468297
For example, when I post on a board with flags (/sp/), it becomes very clear very quickly that foreigners posting on this website hate the United States at an irrational level. They have almost no interactions with Americans in real life and their only exposure to the United States is through media, mostly online.

So when they opine on the United States, our culture, our sports, our education, our technology/business/economics, or even just our daily lives, they often do it from a place of pure resentment, spite, and almost no actual knowledge of the United States whatsoever. Everything they spew about the United States is either propaganda from whatever retarded state-run media they're forced to adhere to, or vile hearsay from some social media figure they've latched onto (also propaganda btw).

Although /sp/'s average IQ level is (maybe?) below this board's average, I have no doubt the foreigners here share the same resentment towards the United States and do not post about American companies/business from a neutral perspective.

I like hearing foreign perspectives by the way, but I deserve to know when I'm speaking with a foreigner, in order to put the things I'm reading into perspective. For example, OP's ESL-syntax plus the content of the post was a dead giveaway that he's posting this out of pure resentment.

62468346
Glad I didn’t buy space x stock

62468390
>>62468335
Fair points, but whenever possible, I try to deal with people in good faith (its a white trait, or flaw, depending on the situation)
The only way out of a low trust civilization is to deal with others in good faith, and violently expelling them permanently if they step out of line. Sadly, we can't do the latter half of that anymore and so here we are

62468428
>>62468297
>>62468335
What? Sorry I couldn't hear you I think you're breaking out? KRRSSHRHSHH KRRSHH...hat? Sorry could yoKRRRSCHH KRRSCH...peat that?

62468437
Holding a bag of spcx $210

62468446
>>62468297
Totally agree. I just assume all other posters are Indian as a default at this point. Mods complain about spam but won't do the one thing that would kill spam forever. Flags and range bans.

62468568
>>62468297
Nigga, your national debt is owned by Asian and Middle Eastern shitholes. Shut your bitch mulatto mouth.

62468589
>>62468568
>Nigga
>your national debt

ESL talking like a nigger. Another reason we need flags.

62468596
>>62468335
I lived in the USA for most of a decade and I hate you just as much

62468632
>>62468596
I don't remember asking, but thanks for your input. I'll add it to the list of reasons we need flags.

62468768
>>62466527
He boghted the spacesex ponzi ? kek

62468783
>>62466527
SPCX sisters?

62468833
>>62468568
25% is foreign owned in the form of bonds. Read a book

62468851
>>62466548
you know all the big news agencies of your country are state media right? It's just more covert

62468863
>62466546
>warned for troll posting
CCP mods are having a melty that someone dared to denounce grorious Chinese ingenuity

62468864
>>62466905
>They're only good at copying
Americans will say this while replying on a Chinese manufactured phone, then have an oolong tea, hit reply, put the phone down, and then eat a Chinese Boy Piss Egg with chopsticks and say we don't make anything new.

62468867
>>62468851
Such as? Name them, cunt.

62468872
>>62468864
Are you ok?

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 140 replies — 91.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 140 • Updated: 2026-07-10 21:59 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62460566
Jugaadjeet

62460624
>>62449441
Bloody bitch bastard

62460700
>>62449388
this is old news
I do this.
>18 months later mattress topper starts to hurt my back
>Whats the reason for the return?
>it hurts my back
>ok thank you sir, here is full refund

or the guy who returned his inflatable pool after a few months. He got the pool for his pet penguin that he ordered. after a few months and no penguin delivery he realized it was a scam and returned the pool and other things he got for the penguin.
>reason for return
>penguin order was a scam, no penguin arrived

I also know some dudes who buy wavestorms, go fuk em up, and exchange them

Costco puts the onus of returns on the manufacturer or supplier as a price for getting on the shelf in the store. just having a spot in costco is worth it enough to most companies to front all the returns for costco's lax return policy. Which is why costco DGAF and rather have customers.

62460720
>>62458144
serious question
I went bowling last week. There was a huge group of brown men. Enjoying themselves, very much, maybe too much.
They can not keep their hands off each other it was the strangest thing I've every seen.
Rubbing, patting, petting....
Each time 1 went to bowl they had to break free of all these hands and arms.
It was like a race to be the last one to touch the bowler, right up until they've thrown. like the hands and arms in drag me to hell expect brown and sensual
so bizarre.
why do they do this?
pic rel but brown and stinky

62460768
>>62455914
I for one love my country. And like true love I hate all those that threaten to jeopardize it. Enjoy your stay but you have to go back

62460799
fun fact: indians skin is brown because they each eat up to 4lbs of poop daily, like how flamingos turn pink from their diet of shrimp!

62460937
>>62460799
Oh I thought flamingos turned pink because they ate pussy

62461696
saaaaaaaaaaaars

62462254
>>62460799
wtf

62462262
>>62449388
All these office chairs are basically painted fabric. The paint flakes off after a while. They shouldn't be able to sell this junk in the first place.

62462271
>>62449388
>People
americans are not people.

62462287
>>62449441
Why do Indians talk so weird? It's like you can immediately tell they're jeets just from text alone

62462621
>>62462271
Seethe

62462950
>>62449388
I've seen people abuse their return program, and get their membership canceled.

62462960
>>62453201
i would cover her face with semen if you catch my drift

62463582
>>62449441
BENCHOOOOOD!!!!!!! WHY YOU REDEEM!! NO NO DO NOT CLICK ON THAT!!!! NOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!

62463635
>>62458606
cucks here won't do shit, they bitch and complain while patronizing Indian establishments. They will talk about the take over and then also about their favorite totally awesome pajeet they met at signhortons. The most opposition you will get is from white canadians. People will whine about the state of tim hortons while simultaneously pretending a coffee from there is a mandatory purchase they will die without

Sad, but they will have to watch canada just disintegrate with no option to fix it before they maybe realize. This whole country is full of just chicken shit low IQ cattle. Maybe 4% of canadians realize how bad this actually is and are doing the bare minimum like not hiring and giving money to the invaders, the rest are fully, entirely complicit in it. If it wasn't for how big this place was, that you can find thousands of acres with not a single person within 5km of you, I would have already left.

62464135
>>62463582
Saaars

62464324
>>62463635
Canada is just India now accept it

62464711
>>62462960
Kek

62464984
>>62449441
kek'd at the replies

62464997
>>62449441
I fucking hate Indians so much man

62465006
>>62449846
lol. Source?

62465010
>>62449441
easy (you)s

62465617
>>62465010
Cope

62465950
>>62449388
+1000 izzat

62466351
saaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaar

62467929
ssaaaaaaaaars!

62468640
>>62465950

62468814
>>62449388
went yesterday, some pajeeta was loading a plastic bag with ice from the soda machine

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 132 replies — 65.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 132 • Updated: 2026-07-10 21:31 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

62454511
>>62426638
this but unironically

62454976
I sold at 115k so no more plans for me.

62455432
>>62454976
Ok

62455827
>>62426535
How you doing now, bobo?

62459212
>>62455827
Lmao

62459912
>>62444587
I like the idea behind their TBV, as it helps to keep all transactions on the BTC chain

62463121
>>62450912
Depends on you

62464112
>>62459912

62466089
>>62450895
ofcourze.. keep accumulating

62466111
Epstein pedo coin radioactive garbage

62466317
I'm just sticking to DCA instead of trying to catch the exact bottom. Every cycle has these pullbacks and guessing the lowest entry usually ends up costing more than just buying consistently. I'm curious how Candora's Liquidity Mirror will show how liquidity changes around key price levels once it launches.

62466320
Just get it through your head that it’s down until Trump is out bare minimum and then it entirely depends on whatever degree of a travesty the next administration is. Trump’s presidency is a bust at this point though. He’s going to fuck shit up and run insider trading scams on the market until the party is over.

62466322
I'd rather wait until BTC shows some actual strength before adding more. Catching falling knives sounds good until it doesn't. Candora's Anti-Spoof feature is expected to help distinguish more consistent liquidity from orders that disappear quickly once it launches.

62466325
Depends on what's driving the move. If it's just leverage getting flushed then I'm not too worried, but if macro conditions keep getting worse I'd expect more downside first. I'm interested in how Candora Agent will help analyze market events alongside price action once it launches.

62466346
I'm buying small but keeping plenty of cash on the side. Nobody knows where the bottom is, so position sizing matters more than making the perfect call. Candora's Slippage Protection will help reduce the impact of unexpected execution price changes once it launches.

62466355
The more everyone starts calling for a crash, the more I start looking for buying opportunities. Fear usually creates better entries than hype. I'm curious how Candora's Order Shield will briefly evaluate sudden price spikes before triggering stop orders once it launches.

62467277
>>62466346
>>62466355
Are you okay?

62467279
>>62426512
BUY the freaking DIP!!!!!!

62468736
>>62467279
fuck buy

item.title: I've invested $40K in ICP since 2022 — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've invested $40K in ICP since 2022

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-07-10 23:24 UTC

OP

My stack of ICP is now worth about $10K. AMA

Early replies (first 3)

62463800
Why would i ask a retard a question? That was rhetorical btw, so dont say "you just did".

62463814
>>62463800
I admit I'm retarded. I'm sharing my experience so I can help other potential retards avoid doing what I did.

62463818
>>62463789
Are you going to buy more? How does it feel to torture yourself like that?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463844
>>62463818
I'm done. It hurt to realize that Dom and his team are mega-scammers who have disdain for those who bought into their vision for the World Computer

62464693
>>62463844
he's being (((suppressed)))

62464870
>>62463789
I've invested $55k with my current holdings being worth ~$17k.

62465954
I still haven’t seen a thread where someone claims that they gave up and sold their bags. The bottom is not in.

62465994
>My stack of ICP is now worth about $10K.
>AMA
okay
why

62466207
>>62463789
Why are you still holding? Didn't icy pee launch their magical AI site generator and it flopped hard?
What is the future prospect of ICP? Just rising tide from macros?

62466279
>>62463789
My losses are worse

62466283
>>62466207
They are coming out with a new Dex called “MultiDex” it’s suppose to be the ultimate dex that will connect every chain and coin, also caffeine AI continues to improve

62466292
>>62466279
>14K pee
what in the dom hell...
why??

62466303
>>62466292
I believed and DCA’d for a while. I could ape in now and get like 10x my stack but I am too demoralized on ICP to buy anymore. I figure this is a make it stack if it becomes a narrative next bull otherwise I don’t care if I lose rest of my money at this point

62466630
>>62463789
caffeine is an extremely shitty ripoff of replit which is already the most ripped off tech product of all time..

62468845
>>62463789
Hi jerry.

62468951
I wasted $2.5k gambling on futures this year and I thought I had it bad. Fucking hell, Anons.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 69 replies — 61.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-07-10 22:25 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62467718
>>62466865
>They go to the nodes, dumbass. That's the whole point of payment abstraction: to pay the nodes.
Problem is, we don't know how much revenue offchain is coming in, we don't know how much or when that revenue is being converted. For instance, I'm planning on budgeting ~$700 a month to use CRE mainnet, how much of this goes to the node operators? When does it convert to Link to pay node operators? How do I know the node operators aren't being stiffed on payments?

>>62466865
>Jesus Christ, why are you even trying to open your big yap about this when you haven't even read the reserve announcement from a year ago?
Because I'm only talking about actual Chainlink services, not that marketing/charity fraud that they push despite dumping 16M link into the market back in October.

62467748
>>62467718
>Problem is, we don't know how much revenue offchain is coming in
That has nothing to do with that calculation, which is entirely based on pure node revenue.

>I'm only talking about actual Chainlink services
You thought PAL only applied to SVR, which hasn't been true for a year.

Stop posting and go do your due diligence.

62467786
>>62467551
I don't think there's any way to track usage or buying pressure coming from this new functionality

62467788
>>62467657
So private node operators get the cash and token holders get nothing. Got it, thanks for clarification

62467795
>>62467788
KEK you want nodes to share their rewards with you simply for holding?

62467799
>>62467619
Then ideally everyone is forced to go to market to buy Link to access the network.

>>62467748
>That has nothing to do with that calculation, which is entirely based on pure node revenue.
It has everything to do with that calculation because dismisses it concedes to the claim that link is decoupled from network usage. You can already access the Link node network just buy paying Chainlink Labs out right from CRE to CCIP (where the fee tokens are sitting in a contract and have yet to be converted to Link) to even Data Streams with that circulating video of the new price changes. If CLL is jumping up the prices and the payments made to the node operators are stagnant, they're getting stiffed out of revenue that CLL is pocketing themselves. Those other services again, PA isn't integrated so CLL is paying from their wallets instead of sourcing Link from the market. This removes buy pressure the network would exert on the market and thus decouples the price from the network usage.

>>62467748
>You thought PAL only applied to SVR, which hasn't been true for a year.
Is me ignoring the Chainlink Reserve really a gotcha you want to die on? Do you really think buying $1M a week really matters? When they dumped 16M Link back in October into the market?

62467812
>>62467799
You're that verbose cripple shill, aren't you?

62467900
>>62467812
I was in the same room when this was announced.

62467944
>>62467799
More words pls

62467948
>>62467563
>>do you really think adoption by the biggest financial institutions in the world is going to create buying pressure?


bro are you still in 2021? they aren't going to buy your shitcoin. banks will never, at any time, buy altcoins. how do you not understand that...

62467960
>>62467786
>I don't think there's any way to track usage or buying pressure coming from this new functionality


is that a joke? of course you can track it lmao... what fucking business doesn't track this stuff?

i think what you meant to say is there's no way they would release this info publicly (because it's so small)

62467965
>>62467795
Isn’t that what staking was suppose to be. My god, this is embarrassing

62467972
>>62467795
>duuuuuuuuuude how dare you ask for return on investment?!


you don't see many of these c-u-c-k-h-o-l-d-s left here anymore, but every now and then an old timer pops up


their rage is created when they see someone else break free from the cult, but can't do it themselves. this anger is projected outwards, but the reality is they're pissed off at hearing the reminders of how they're being grifted. by attempting to shame detractors from asking questions, they secretly hope these reminders will stop, and they can go back to their fantasy of being rich one day

62467979
>>62467972

Kek fuddie

62467988
>>62467965
>staking
>holding
pick one

>>62467900
Listen to yourself, you're whining about the fact that nodes may not be getting paid enough.
Chainlink's node operator list is absolutely full of companies built entirely around mining, staking, ... all kinds of other cryptos.
They would know when they weren't getting paid enough for their work.

62468309
>>62467988
>Listen to yourself, you're whining about the fact that nodes may not be getting paid enough.

Listen to yourself, you've avoided addressing my criticism about link being disassociated from network usage and instead twisted my points and resorted to name calling as if you're a paid shill. Are you paid by Chainlink? No? Then why not honestly argue against my point then? You're invested in Chainlink right? The price is in shambles while Chainlink has every accolade and partnership every other crypto dreams of. Why ignore the elephant in the room?

62468349
>>62467587
Where can I get a Pepe action figure?

62468366
>>62467551
>your max upside in next 3 years here is a 3x but you could get that with bitcoin, sol, and prob eth with substantially less risk
Show us your portfolio YTD Adem, and show us the 5 year return as well. Let's have a laugh. Go on, show us.

>>62467718
This is the XRP broken-in verbose shill kek.
>>62467900
>my dad works for nintendo
sure thing unc

62468526
>>62468366
>sure thing unc

62468535
>>62468526
>bragging about getting conned

62468607
>>62466449
>15th
Fuck, I can't wait that long. I can't bear to go back to work on Monday, I'm selling everything right now

62468628
>>62466927
>if DTCC alone uses Chainlink across their entire throughput and fees
which would never ever happen kek, you'll be lucky for them to use it for .001% of their shit. Link is a novelty gimmick for swift and dtcc, what do linkies not get about this?

62468726
>>62466927
Great for chainlink Labs if true.
0 will flow to the funding token you hold

62468785
>>62468309
If inflationary rewards "disassociate price from network usage" then that's true for pretty much all cryptos that have ever existed.
Starting with the first one to ever exist.

>>62468366
>This is the XRP broken-in verbose shill kek.
Yup, obvious as ever.

>>62468726
Anon, that calculation is based on node revenue in Link.

62468797
>>62468785
>node revenue in Link
They sell link to cover costs
Cll sells link to cover costs and they will continue to sell till 2030, after that they will sell the reserve

62468800
>>62468797
and then they will mint new tokens lmao

62468801
>>62468797
>They sell link to cover costs
All crypto nodes do. Why is that a problem suddenly for Link?

62468803
>>62468801
cuz it leads to link being worth $7 stinkie

62468805
>>62468803
No, this does

62468852
>>62468628
If it ends up being cheaper, faster, more efficient with less friction than legacy systems, they have no logical reason to not deploy the technology everywhere they can

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 90 replies — 44.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 90 • Updated: 2026-07-10 21:30 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463258
What's the best thing to do? Buy or sell

62463262
>>62450728

62463263
>>62463201
WBTC TVL is 10x more than all of these combined. There's way more BTC lending happening on Ethereum than on BTC L2s.

62463265
>>62450728
ETF’s = whales

62463279
>>62459225
You are forgetting every nations currency have the strength of wet noodles now. They will print again soon.

62463285
bitcoin is a commodity backed ETF without the commodity

all it ever is and all it ever will be

62463301
There should be a 90s themed live action series called something cheesy like "Good morning Mr. Nakamoto" about the bitcoin creator. After making it big in his mid 20s he has become a multi billionaire, but is extremely paranoid so he decided to keep a low profile. Now he's in his late 30s, a family man with a couple kids, and the series follow an episodic format where he desperately tries to hide from his family his enormous wealth and just pretends to be an office worker. The thing is he has zero credentials so he can't actually get a job so it becomes incrementally difficult (due to plot inducement stupidity) to maintain the farce. Prying yet endearing secondary characters keep coming and leaving, and oftentimes misunderstandings make him almost blow his cover. During some weaker seasons he's stalked by some group of nerds that are almost sure he's the real deal and want to take revenge due to prices plunging, but eventually some shenanigan makes the nerds pin their suspicions on Nakamoto's rival (who was trying to cuck him with his wife, who by the way goes through a very annoying arc because she realizes his hubby is not being open with her or something). Nakamoto's personality is a bit like Hal from Malcolm in the middle but less lolrandom, he just plays as a fool to better hide his secret.

62463320
>>62463301
Some thematic episodes could include his wife hiring a financial advisor because he played the broke ass bit too much, and he tries to stop the advisor from peeking at his 100b net worth at any cost. He also gets in trouble because the advisor is not very bright and tells terrible advice very overconfidently, so Nakamoto counters with suspiciously good and sharp analysis (fortunately his wife just doesnt care because he has ingrained the dumb with money narrative and the advisor is very confident, and also a financial advisor duh). In another episode he gets pinned by the IRS after accidentally doing some billion level transaction and having to pay tenths of millions in taxes. Another one where he (again, Plot) is revealed to have all the money behind a 20 word seed, but he doesnt remember the words, so it's a musical where he finally manages to recall them through a catchy song. The episode is rated quite poorly though because it doesn't fit with the general tone. In the seventh and last season his younger kid finds out by dumb luck and the rest of that season is him preventing them from telling mom. The show gets cancelled afterwards due do decline in viewership and the plot never gets finished.

62463429
>>62463301
>>62463320
This has potential

62464033
>>62463263
but it doesn't mean it'll stay that way.

62464098
>>62463429
I could foresee an actual ending where he has to let all his earnings go because of some contrived dumb reason, but it's framed in a way that looks somewhat deep and poignant. His cover is blown in the process, but as he's revealed to be the creator of Bitcoin, he suddently gets a call from one of the companies he tried to apply the most though all the seasons (let's say it's a mid-sized company that offers an average salary, it has it's wacky employees and boss, they usually appear for like 10 minutes every few episodes just to reject Nakamoto). He's finally accepted and he merrily joins (it's a subtle joke because now that he's a public figure he could easily be gunning for 500k jobs, but he settles for this as a nudge to the audience), managing to achieve the life he pretended to have (and actually wanted) from the get go. The series end with him walking into the office's slightly rundown and shabby building with a bright smile. You even get to make that cheap overused allegory of ''choosing happiness and being true to your principles over riches''. I feel like there's an alternate universe where this would have killed it.

62464115
>>62463256
I'll keep testing TBVs on Aave V4. BTC has a lot more going on now than just being a store of value.

62464121
>>62463301
The best ending would be nobody ever finding out if it's really him. Keeping the mystery alive is probably the most Satoshi thing possible.

62464142
>>62464098
You're too invested in that drama. It's not happening in real life.

62464251
>>62464121
You know what? That's fair. I relied too much on 90s sitcom tropes. It should just end episodically, there's just no more seasons and that's it.
>>62464142
I don't have much going on in my life and my family and friends find my occurrences boring. And it's supposed to be a comedy. What drama? I want it to be goofy and for all publics!

62464760
>>62464033
Take stablecoins for another example. Zero. Zilch. Nada. $0.00 -- Remember when 100% of USDT used to be on Bitcoin? Now it's 0%, Omni is dead and Tether has moved to every other chain. There is nothing more to Bitcoin than BTC the asset.

62464820
I have a credit card that gives me cash back in the form of BTC. So my investment strategy is just skimming off my purchases anyway.

62465005
>>62464251
Why do you think you're right about that and you think you're so sure about it?

62465089
>>62465005
I don't understand the question. Right about what.

62466102
>>62464115
Cool. There's clearly more happening around bitcoin now than there was a few years ago.

62466109
>>62464760
I'm talking about native BTC, not stablecoins. Those are two different discussions.

62466120
Imagine having to pay for each website you visited…that’s how stupid it is for proof of work, btc will never work long term (already sucks now)

62466195
>>62459656
Well this is a link board so I won't say anything negative. I hope link goes to 10k eoy for all the anons holding it.

62466244
>>62466102
You do realize Aave is on Ethereum, right? It's great for BTC the asset but not so great for Bitcoin the network. When you're saying innovation isn't happening on Bitcoin base layer but "around it", do you mean on Aave for example?
>>62466109
And I was talking about innovation on Bitcoin in general. Originally I replied to
>why are people still building new infrastructure around it?
saying there's no infrastructure, well... besides bridging BTC to Ethereum. Saying there's no stablecoin infra on top of Bitcoin is relevant to the discussion.

62466717
>>62466244
Bridging is risky

62466757
>>62466195
Chinlink

62467179
>>62466120
What drugs you're taking ser?

62467263
>>62466195
buy link

62467380
>>62463301
Is this real

62468734
>>62463320
I remember this .how time flies

item.title: XRP and SWIFT going live. XRP settling cross-border payments on a network that moves $150 trillion annually. The old rai… — 14 replies — 19.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP and SWIFT going live. XRP settling cross-border payments on a network that moves $150 trillion annually. The old rai…

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-10 22:48 UTC

OP

XRP and SWIFT going live.

XRP settling cross-border payments on a network that moves $150 trillion annually. The old rails are not being replaced — they are being upgraded with XRP running through them.

Early replies (first 3)

62467887
>>62467875
sounds good to me. prove it and ill buy some

62467913
"Crypto researcher" More like fucking idiot.

Whos paying these people to lie like this?

62467922
Markets must frontrunning this like crazy, lemme check - awwww, shit....

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62468113
>>62467875
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62468138
>>62467913
That emdash

62468148
>>62467875
hmm strange...

62468367
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62468544
>>62468148
Lol I fucking love Tom. He mindbroke so many XRP fags.

62468622
>>62468544
but theyre so fucking stupid that they think hes under NDA and has to lie.

62468735
>>62468622
>>62468544
The funniest part is him getting yeeted out of Swift.

62468758
>>62468735
Haha that'll teach him, XRP RUNNING THINGS FROM THE INSIDE, KABOOOOOOM!!!

62468828
>>62467875
BOOOOOOOM!!

62468887
>>62467875
SWIFT CAN TONGUE PUNCH MY TURD CUTTER, I DON'T WAN'T THEM ANY WHERE NEER XRP. HNNNNNNNNNN!

62468893
I love xrp and i want them in competition with swift not working with them. I might dump my stack if this happens. Fuck swift.
